speaker: PERICLES
text: Hail, Dian! To perform thy just command,

speaker: PERICLES
text: I here confess myself the King of Tyre,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who, frighted from my country, did wed

speaker: PERICLES
text: At Pentapolis the fair Thaisa.

speaker: PERICLES
text: At sea in childbed died she, but brought forth

speaker: PERICLES
text: A maid child called Marina, whom, O goddess,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Wears yet thy silver livery. She at Tarsus

speaker: PERICLES
text: Was nursed with Cleon, who at fourteen years

speaker: PERICLES
text: He sought to murder. But her better stars

speaker: PERICLES
text: Brought her to Mytilene, ’gainst whose shore riding,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Her fortunes brought the maid aboard us, where,

speaker: PERICLES
text: By her own most clear remembrance, she made known

speaker: PERICLES
text: Herself my daughter.

speaker: THAISA
text: Voice and favor!

speaker: THAISA
text: You are, you are—O royal Pericles!

speaker: PERICLES
text: What means the nun? She dies! Help, gentlemen!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Noble sir,

speaker: CERIMON
text: If you have told Diana’s altar true,

speaker: CERIMON
text: This is your wife.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Reverend appearer, no.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I threw her overboard with these very arms.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Upon this coast, I warrant you.

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Tis most certain.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Look to the lady. O, she’s but overjoyed.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Early one blustering morn this lady was

speaker: CERIMON
text: Thrown upon this shore. I oped the coffin,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Found there rich jewels, recovered her, and placed her

speaker: CERIMON
text: Here in Diana’s temple.

speaker: PERICLES
text: May we see them?

speaker: CERIMON
text: Great sir, they shall be brought you to my house,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Whither I invite you. Look, Thaisa

speaker: CERIMON
text: Is recoverèd.

speaker: THAISA
text: O, let me look!

speaker: THAISA
text: If he be none of mine, my sanctity

speaker: THAISA
text: Will to my sense bend no licentious ear,

speaker: THAISA
text: But curb it, spite of seeing.—O, my lord,

speaker: THAISA
text: Are you not Pericles? Like him you spake,

speaker: THAISA
text: Like him you are. Did you not name a tempest,

speaker: THAISA
text: A birth and death?

speaker: PERICLES
text: The voice of dead Thaisa!

speaker: THAISA
text: That Thaisa am I, supposèd dead

speaker: THAISA
text: And drowned.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Immortal Dian!

speaker: THAISA
text: Now I know you better.

speaker: THAISA
text: When we with tears parted Pentapolis,

speaker: THAISA
text: The king my father gave you such a ring.

speaker: PERICLES
text: This, this! No more, you gods! Your present kindness

speaker: PERICLES
text: Makes my past miseries sports. You shall do well

speaker: PERICLES
text: That on the touching of her lips I may

speaker: PERICLES
text: Melt and no more be seen.—O, come, be buried

speaker: PERICLES
text: A second time within these arms!

speaker: MARINA
text: My heart

speaker: MARINA
text: Leaps to be gone into my mother’s bosom.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Look who kneels here, flesh of thy flesh, Thaisa,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thy burden at the sea, and called Marina

speaker: PERICLES
text: For she was yielded there.

speaker: THAISA
text: Blessed, and mine own!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Hail, madam, and my queen.

speaker: THAISA
text: I know you not.

speaker: PERICLES
text: You have heard me say, when I did fly from Tyre

speaker: PERICLES
text: I left behind an ancient substitute.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Can you remember what I called the man?

speaker: PERICLES
text: I have named him oft.

speaker: THAISA
text: ’Twas Helicanus then.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Still confirmation!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Embrace him, dear Thaisa. This is he.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Now do I long to hear how you were found,

speaker: PERICLES
text: How possibly preserved, and who to thank,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Besides the gods, for this great miracle.

speaker: THAISA
text: Lord Cerimon, my lord, this man

speaker: THAISA
text: Through whom the gods have shown their power, that can

speaker: THAISA
text: From first to last resolve you.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Reverend sir,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The gods can have no mortal officer

speaker: PERICLES
text: More like a god than you. Will you deliver

speaker: PERICLES
text: How this dead queen relives?

speaker: CERIMON
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Beseech you, first go with me to my house,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Where shall be shown you all was found with her,

speaker: CERIMON
text: How she came placed here in the temple,

speaker: CERIMON
text: No needful thing omitted.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Pure Dian, I bless thee for thy vision, and

speaker: PERICLES
text: Will offer night oblations to thee.—Thaisa,

speaker: PERICLES
text: This prince, the fair betrothèd of your daughter,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Shall marry her at Pentapolis.—And now this ornament

speaker: PERICLES
text: Makes me look dismal will I clip to form,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And what this fourteen years no razor touched,

speaker: PERICLES
text: To grace thy marriage day I’ll beautify.

speaker: THAISA
text: Lord Cerimon hath letters of good credit, sir,

speaker: THAISA
text: My father’s dead.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Heavens make a star of him! Yet there, my queen,

speaker: PERICLES
text: We’ll celebrate their nuptials, and ourselves

speaker: PERICLES
text: Will in that kingdom spend our following days.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Our son and daughter shall in Tyrus reign.—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Lord Cerimon, we do our longing stay

speaker: PERICLES
text: To hear the rest untold. Sir, lead ’s the way.
